name: phase3-debug-vision
description: Guide for debugging Nova Pro vision assessment issues

action.message:
Debug Phase 3 vision assessment issues. Check in this order:

1. VISION MODEL ACCESS — verify Nova Pro is accessible:
   Check VISION_MODEL in .env (default: us.amazon.nova-pro-v1:0)
   Check AWS_REGION in .env (default: us-east-1)
   Verify IAM permissions include bedrock:InvokeModel for the vision model

2. TIMEOUT ISSUES — if vision checks are slow:
   VISION_TIMEOUT default is 4.0 s. Increase in .env: VISION_TIMEOUT=8.0
   Timeout always returns 'continue' — execution is never blocked

3. ALWAYS CONTINUING — if vision never replans or aborts:
   Check confidence threshold — VisionAssessor uses temperature=0.1
   Check _SYSTEM_PROMPT bias rules — default is conservative (continue unless imminent collision)
   Check that instruction contains 'stop when' or 'avoid' keywords for non-continue decisions

4. TOO MANY REPLANS — if vision replans excessively:
   Reduce MAX_REPLANS in .env (default: 3)
   Increase VISION_MIN_STEP to skip more short steps (default: 0.5 s)

5. VISION SKIPPING ALL STEPS — if no vision checks run:
   Check _should_assess() — returns False for steps < VISION_MIN_STEP_SECS
   Check _has_vision — must be True (requires CameraPolicy, not NovaPolicy)
   Verify --vision flag used in main.py

6. JSON PARSE ERRORS — if VisionAssessor logs 'JSON parse failure':
   Nova Pro returned non-JSON. Check raw_response in logs.
   _parse_decision() strips markdown fences — if model adds other formatting, update the regex.

Report what you find.
